channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Sotomayor says presidents have largely followed rule of law, offers words of caution
item.description: U.S. Supreme Court Justice Sonia Sotomayor said presidents have historically obeyed judicial rulings with a few exceptions while advocating for the court to move cautiously to maintain a system of checks and balances.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DOGE cuts $900 million from agency that tracks American students' academic progress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal research agency that tracks the progress of America’s students is being hit with almost $900 million in cuts after Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency found no need for much of its work.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court rejects Trump administration push to reinstate spending freezes on grants and loans
item.description: A federal appeals court on Tuesday rejected a Trump administration push to reinstate a sweeping pause on federal funding, a decision that comes after a judge found the administration had not fully obeyed an earlier order.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: With federal money at risk, Minnesota’s public colleges stress high stakes of state budget debate
item.description: The University of Minnesota and Minnesota State Colleges and Universities systems are hoping for extra allocations from the Legislature this year. They know it’s a challenge given the state’s leaner budget.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Scientists expect major 'medical breakthroughs' despite Trump's cap on NIH research funding
item.description: The Trump administration's move to slash overhead costs associated with federally funded research has generated immense backlash, but doctors who spoke to Fox News Digital praised the move, arguing it will help "optimize" how taxpayer dollars are used when it comes to scientific resea
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge adjusts ruling blocking Musk, DOGE from Treasury Department payment systems
item.description: A federal judge Tuesday clarified that a ruling preventing Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing the Treasury Department’s payment systems does not extend to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada lawmaker moves to put Indigenous Peoples' Day on Columbus Day
item.description: Nevada law says Indigenous Peoples Day falls on Aug. 9, but a lawmaker is trying to change that with AB144, which would set the observation on the second Monday in October — the same day as Colombus Day.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Senator seeks watchdog inquiry into Kash Patel, alleges behind-the-scenes role in purge at FBI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A top Democratic senator has asked the Justice Department's inspector general to investigate after he says he received information that President Donald Trump's pick to lead the FBI, Kash Patel, had been “personally directing the ongoing purge” of agents at the bureau.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth says DOGE welcome at Pentagon as Defense Department reviews military posture globally
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Pentagon did not push same "globalist agendas" as USAID but welcomed DOGE "partnership" to find billions in waste.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House fires USAID inspector general after warning about funding oversight, officials say
item.description: The White House fired the inspector general for the U.S. Agency for International Development on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nearly half of NY voters don’t want Mayor Adams’ charges dropped: poll
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Nearly half of New Yorkers oppose Mayor Eric Adams’ charges being dropped, according to a recent PIX11 poll.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: King Abdullah says Jordan will bring in 2K 'very ill' Palestinian children
item.description: Trump is not backing down from his plans for the future of Gaza and is pressuring Jordan, Egypt and other Arab countries to take in Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Vance to attend international AI and security summits as Ukraine war and tariffs loom
item.description: Vance will likely use the summits to resist further AI regulation while reiterating a common Trump refrain: that military allies need to further ramp up spending that supports NATO and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx, MPR News

item.title: Trump pardons ex-Illinois Gov. Rod Blagojevich 5 years after commuting his sentence
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday pardoned former Illinois Gov. Rod Blagojevich, whose 14-year sentence for political corruption charges he commuted during his first term.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he’s in ‘no rush’ to develop Gaza
item.description: “Think of it as a big real estate site, and the United States is going to own it, and will slowly, very slowly, we’re in no rush, in development,” Trump told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: DETR says unemployment claim backlog is gone, answers questions on call wait times, AI
item.description: A backlog of unemployment claims that soared as high as 45,000 cases during the pandemic has been cleared, officials told lawmakers on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tampa Bay lawmaker pushes bold legislation to protect homeownership from corporations
item.description: In what's being called a "bold new measure," one Tampa Bay lawmaker wants to put families over corporate elites by making sure local Floridians can stay in their neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Renters' rights and 13 more bills to watch at the 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: Lawmakers are busy in Carson City drawing up new rules to live by, including tougher penalties for committing crimes, protections for transgender Nevadans and a variety of laws related to housing and renters' rights.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
